name: Serbia and Montenegro (1992-2006)
description: Three equal horizontal stripes — blue over white over red — covering the field.
history: Adopted on 27 April 1992 as the flag of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, the rump state formed by Serbia and Montenegro after the dissolution of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia. The design returned to the plain blue-white-red Pan-Slavic tricolour of the pre-war Kingdom of Yugoslavia, dropping the red star of the SFR Yugoslavia. The state was renamed Serbia and Montenegro on 4 February 2003. The flag remained in use until the Montenegrin independence referendum of 21 May 2006, after which Serbia and Montenegro became separate states on 3 June 2006. Serbia subsequently adopted a flag with the same colours but with the Serbian coat of arms; Montenegro adopted a wholly different design.

features.symbolism: the Pan-Slavic colours, continuing the tradition of the Kingdom of Yugoslavia tricolour with a different stripe order
